hero.title: Run a whole sprint through one QA pipeline
hero.activeBody: {{meta}} — in the {{status}} stage. Review cases, run Playwright in parallel, and publish evidence back to every ticket.
hero.emptyBody: Select tickets, batch-generate test cases, review like pull requests, run Playwright in parallel, then publish evidence back to every ticket.

recentActivity.title: Recent activity
recentActivity.empty: No activity yet — actions you take will show up here.

recentRuns.title: Recent runs
recentRuns.empty: No runs yet — create one to get started.

hero.title: Getting Started
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to go from a backlog of tickets to reviewed, automated, executed, and evidenced test runs. Skim it, or jump straight in with the tour or a live sample run.

whatIs.title: What is Q-Agent?
whatIs.body1: Q-Agent is an AI-native QA operating system. It turns the tickets in your backlog into reviewed, automated, executed, and evidenced test runs — end to end. Instead of writing test cases and Playwright scripts by hand, you point Q-Agent at your work items and codebase; it drafts the cases, you review them, and it generates the automation, runs it, captures the evidence, and reports back to the tickets.
whatIs.body2: You stay in control at every step — the AI proposes, you approve. Nothing is created in your provider until you say so.

projects.title: Projects
projects.body: A product or repo you test. Each project wires up its work-item source and the codebase Q-Agent generates automation against.

tickets.title: Tickets
tickets.body: Work items synced live from Azure DevOps or Jira. They're the raw input the AI reads to draft test cases.

runs.title: Runs
runs.body: A batch QA session over a set of selected tickets. A run carries its own cases, automation, execution results, and evidence.

knowledgeBases.title: Knowledge bases
knowledgeBases.body: Per-repo indexed context. Q-Agent reads your code so the cases and Playwright specs it writes fit how your app actually works.

pipelineIntro.title: Eight stages, one run
pipelineIntro.body: Every run moves through the same pipeline — the exact stages you'll see in the run workspace sidebar. Each one hands off to the next.

syncSelect.label: Sync & Select
syncSelect.what: Pull the latest tickets from your provider and pick the ones this run should cover.

analyze.label: Analyze
analyze.what: The AI reads each ticket against your knowledge base and drafts candidate test cases.

review.label: Review
review.what: Approve, edit, or reject the AI-generated cases before anything leaves Q-Agent.

link.label: Link
link.what: Create the reviewed cases in your provider and link them back to their source tickets.

automation.label: Automation
automation.what: Generate runnable Playwright specs from the approved cases.

execution.label: Execution
execution.what: Run the generated scripts and collect pass/fail results per case.

evidence.label: Evidence
evidence.what: Capture screenshots, video, and a trace for every executed case.

publish.label: Publish
publish.what: Post the results and evidence back onto the originating tickets.

firstRunIntro.title: From zero to published
firstRunIntro.body: The full happy path, once. After the first time it becomes second nature.

connectProvider.title: Connect a provider
connectProvider.body: In Settings, link your work-item source (ADO or Jira) and the repo you want automation written against.

syncTickets.title: Sync tickets
syncTickets.body: Pull your backlog into Q-Agent. Use Basic or Advanced filters to reflect exactly what you care about.

selectCreateRun.title: Select & create a run
selectCreateRun.body: Pick the tickets to cover, then spin them into a new run — your workspace for this QA session.

reviewCases.title: Review the cases
reviewCases.body: Open Review and approve or refine the AI-drafted cases. Nothing is created upstream until you say so.

linkUpstream.title: Link them upstream
linkUpstream.body: Push the approved cases into your provider and link them to their tickets from the Link stage.

generateAutomation.title: Generate automation
generateAutomation.body: From Automation, turn the cases into Playwright specs tailored to your codebase.

execute.title: Execute
execute.body: Run the scripts from Execution and watch pass/fail results land per case.

reviewEvidence.title: Review evidence & publish
reviewEvidence.body: Inspect the captured screenshots, video, and traces, then Publish the outcome back to the tickets.

connections.title: Provider connections
connections.body: In Settings, connect a work-item source — Azure DevOps or Jira — so Q-Agent can sync tickets and publish results. Connect the repo you want automation generated against so the AI has real code context.

claude.title: Claude credentials
claude.body: Claude credentials are per-user and set in Settings. They power the analysis and generation stages, and they stay private to your account — you manage your own.

credentials.q: Do I need real credentials to explore?
credentials.a: No. Click “Explore the sample run” above to open a fully-populated run with no setup.

rewatchTour.q: How do I re-watch the tour?
rewatchTour.a: Open the command palette (⌘K) and choose “Start product tour” any time.

runsLive.a: Runs are private to you — only you see the runs you create.

editDrafts.q: Can I edit what the AI drafts?
editDrafts.a: Yes. The Review stage exists precisely so you approve or edit every case before it's linked upstream.

localAgent.title: Run suites on your machine

about.title: What is the Local Agent?
about.body: A small CLI you run on your own computer. It claims execution jobs from Q‑Agent and runs Playwright headed, locally — so when a suite needs a manual login (e.g. MFA), the browser opens right where you are. Your session cookies never leave your machine; only progress, results, and screenshots are sent back.

prereq.title: PREREQUISITES
prereq.item1: Nothing to install for the Windows app — Node.js 18+ only if you use the npx option
prereq.item2: Chromium is installed automatically the first time you run the agent

localAgent.nativeAppNote: Native app — no Node required, and it self-updates. With Node 18+ you can instead run npx @q-agent/agent
localAgent.noDevices: No devices paired yet. Click "Add device" to get a pairing command.

pairing.title: Pair a device
pairing.instructions: Open the Local Agent app, type this code and click Connect — the downloaded app already knows this server. Or, from a terminal:

revoke.title: Revoke this device?
revoke.message: "{{name}}" will no longer be able to claim or run jobs. You can pair it again later.

toast.pairCodeFailed: Failed to generate a pairing code
